title: OralMLLM-Bench: Evaluating Cognitive Capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models in Dental Practice
summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have emerged as a promising paradigm for dental image analysis. However, their ability to capture the multi-level cognitive processes required for radiographic analysis remains unclear. Here, we present a comprehensive benchmark to evaluate the cognitive capabilities of MLLMs in dental radiographic analysis. It spans three critical imaging modalities, i.e., periapical, panoramic, and lateral cephalometric radiographs, and defines four cognitive categories: perception, comprehension, prediction, and decision-making. The benchmark comprises 27 clinically grounded tasks derived from public datasets, with manually curated annotations and 3,820 clinician assessments for evaluation. Six frontier MLLMs, including GPT-5.2 and GLM-4.6, are evaluated. We demonstrate the performance gap between MLLMs and clinicians in dental practice, delineate model strengths and limitations, characterize failure patterns, and provide recommendations for improvement. This data resource will facilitate the development of next-generation artificial intelligence systems aligned with clinical cognition, safety requirements, and workflow complexity in dental practice.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一个评估多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在牙科影像分析中认知能力的全面基准，涵盖三类影像模态和四级认知任务，实验表明MLLMs与临床医生之间存在显著性能差距，并指出了模型的优势、局限及改进方向。
AI.motivation: 尽管多模态大语言模型在牙科影像分析中展现出潜力，但它们能否捕捉放射影像分析所需的多层次认知过程尚不明确，目前缺乏系统评估MLLMs在牙科影像中感知、理解、预测和决策等认知能力的基准。
AI.method: 构建了一个覆盖根尖片、全景片和侧位头影测量片三类影像模态的基准，定义了感知、理解、预测和决策四个认知类别，包含27个基于公开数据集的临床任务，并配有手工标注和3,820份临床医生评估，评估了GPT-5.2和GLM-4.6等六种前沿MLLMs。
AI.result: 实验揭示了MLLMs与临床医生在牙科实践中的性能差距，明确了模型的优势和局限性，刻画了失败模式，并给出了改进建议。
AI.conclusion: 本基准为开发与临床认知、安全需求及工作流复杂性相匹配的下一代牙科人工智能系统提供了数据资源和评估框架。

title: Injecting Distributional Awareness into MLLMs via Reinforcement Learning for Deep Imbalanced Regression
summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) struggle with numerical regression under long-tailed target distributions. Token-level supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and point-wise regression rewards bias learning toward high-density regions, leading to regression-to-the-mean behavior and poor tail performance. We identify the lack of cross-sample relational supervision as a key limitation of existing MLLM training paradigms. To address it, we propose a distribution-aware reinforcement learning framework based on Group Relative Policy Optimization, which introduces batch-level comparison-based supervision via the Concordance Correlation Coefficient-based reward to align predicted and ground-truth distributions in terms of correlation, scale, and mean. The framework is plug-and-play, requiring no architectural modification. Experiments on a unified suite of long-tailed regression benchmarks show consistent improvements over SFT and existing MLLM regression methods, with particularly strong gains in medium- and few-shot regimes.

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于分布感知强化学习（DRGRPO）的框架，通过引入CCC奖励和组相对策略优化，在无需修改模型架构的条件下，显著提升了多模态大语言模型在长尾数值回归任务中特别是中低样本区域的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有多模态大语言模型在长尾目标分布下的数值回归任务中表现不佳，基于token的监督微调和逐点回归奖励会导致模型偏向高密度区域、出现回归至均值行为，且尾部样本性能严重退化。当前训练范式缺乏跨样本的关系监督能力是导致该问题的关键局限。
AI.method: 提出一种即插即用的分布感知强化学习框架DRGRPO，基于组相对策略优化（GRPO），引入基于一致性相关系数（CCC）的奖励函数，通过批次级别的对比监督来对齐预测分布与真实分布在相关性、尺度和均值三个维度上的统计特性，无需对模型架构进行任何修改。
AI.result: 在统一的长尾回归基准测试套件上的实验表明，DRGRPO相较于监督微调和现有的多模态大语言模型回归方法取得了一致性提升，尤其在中等样本和少样本场景下取得了显著的性能增益。
AI.conclusion: 该研究揭示了跨样本关系监督对解决长尾回归问题的重要性，验证了基于分布对齐的强化学习范式在多模态大语言模型数值回归任务中的有效性和通用性，为后续无需架构修改的性能提升提供了可借鉴的方向。

title: X2SAM: Any Segmentation in Images and Videos
summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have demonstrated strong image-level visual understanding and reasoning, yet their pixel-level perception across both images and videos remains limited. Foundation segmentation models such as the SAM series produce high-quality masks, but they rely on low-level visual prompts and cannot natively interpret complex conversational instructions. Existing segmentation MLLMs narrow this gap, but are usually specialized for either images or videos and rarely support both textual and visual prompts in one interface. We introduce X2SAM, a unified segmentation MLLM that extends any-segmentation capabilities from images to videos. Given conversational instructions and visual prompts, X2SAM couples an LLM with a Mask Memory module that stores guided vision features for temporally consistent video mask generation. The same formulation supports generic, open-vocabulary, referring, reasoning, grounded conversation generation, interactive, and visual grounded segmentation across image and video inputs. We further introduce the Video Visual Grounded (V-VGD) segmentation benchmark, which evaluates whether a model can segment object tracks in videos from interactive visual prompts. With a unified joint training strategy over heterogeneous image and video datasets, X2SAM delivers strong video segmentation performance, remains competitive on image segmentation benchmarks, and preserves general image and video chat ability.

AI.tldr: X2SAM是一个统一的分割多模态大语言模型，通过引入Mask Memory模块和联合训练策略，将任意分割能力从图像扩展到视频，支持多种文本和视觉提示的分割任务，并在保持图像分割性能和对话能力的同时实现了强大的视频分割性能。
AI.motivation: 现有的多模态大语言模型缺乏像素级感知能力，基础分割模型无法理解复杂对话指令，而现有的分割MLLM通常只专门处理图像或视频，且很少在同一接口中同时支持文本和视觉提示，缺乏一个能统一处理图像和视频多种分割任务的模型。
AI.method: X2SAM将大型语言模型与Mask Memory模块耦合，该模块存储引导视觉特征用于生成时间一致的视频掩码；采用统一的联合训练策略，在异构的图像和视频数据集上联合训练，支持通用、开放词汇、指代、推理、接地对话生成、交互式和视觉接地分割等多种任务。
AI.result: X2SAM在视频分割任务上表现出色，在图像分割基准上保持竞争力，并保留了通用的图像和视频对话能力；论文还引入了Video Visual Grounded (V-VGD)分割基准，用于评估模型从交互式视觉提示中分割视频中物体轨迹的能力。
AI.conclusion: X2SAM证明了通过统一的模型架构和联合训练策略，可以有效地将任意分割能力从图像扩展到视频，同时支持多种分割任务和提示方式，为构建统一的视觉分割基础模型提供了可行路径。

title: Valley3: Scaling Omni Foundation Models for E-commerce
summary: In this work, we present Valley3, an omni multimodal large language model (MLLM) developed for diverse global e-commerce tasks, with unified understanding and reasoning capabilities across text, images, video, and audio. A key feature of Valley3 is its native multilingual audio capability for e-commerce, developed by extending vision-language models to better support crucial audio-visual tasks, particularly in short-video scenarios. To achieve this, we carefully design a four-stage omni e-commerce continued pre-training pipeline, through which Valley3 progressively acquires audio understanding, cross-modal instruction-following, e-commerce domain knowledge, and long-context reasoning capabilities, ultimately evolving into an omni model for diverse e-commerce scenarios. Then, we further improve Valley3 through post-training to encourage long-chain reasoning with controllable reasoning modes, enabling one non-thinking mode and three distinct levels of thinking, thereby balancing inference efficiency in simple scenarios with deep reasoning for complex applications. Moreover, we equip Valley3 with agentic search capabilities to proactively invoke search tools and acquire task-relevant information for e-commerce deep research tasks. To comprehensively assess the capabilities of Valley3, we construct an omni e-commerce benchmark spanning 6 tasks. Experimental results show that Valley3 consistently outperforms strong baselines on our in-house and open-source e-commerce benchmarks, while remaining competitive on general-domain benchmarks.

AI.tldr: Valley3是一个面向全球电商场景的通用多模态大语言模型（MLLM），通过四阶段持续预训练和后训练实现文本、图像、视频、音频的统一理解与推理，在电商和多模态基准上均取得领先性能。
AI.motivation: 现有大语言模型在多模态电商任务中缺乏对音频等模态的原生支持，尤其在短视频场景下难以实现跨模态的理解与推理，且缺少面向电商领域的高效推理可控性与长链推理能力。
AI.method: Valley3采用四阶段电商持续预训练流程，依次获取音频理解、跨模态指令跟随、电商领域知识及长上下文推理能力；后训练阶段引入可控推理模式（一个非推理模式和三级思维链深度），并配备代理搜索能力以主动调用搜索工具获取任务相关信息。
AI.result: 在覆盖6项任务的电商多模态基准评测中，Valley3在内部电商数据集和公开电商基准上均持续优于强基线方法，同时在通用域基准上也保持了有竞争力的性能。
AI.conclusion: Valley3通过渐进式多阶段训练和可控推理机制，有效提升了电商多模态大模型在跨模态理解、领域知识和推理效率之间的平衡，为电商场景下的通用多模态智能体提供了可行的技术路径。

title: TRIP-Evaluate: An Open Multimodal Benchmark for Evaluating Large Models in Transportation
summary: Large language models (LLMs) and multimodal large models (MLLMs) are increasingly used for transportation tasks such as regulation question answering, traffic management support, engineering review, and autonomous-driving scene reasoning. Yet transportation workflows are rule-intensive, computation-intensive, safety-critical, and inherently multimodal. Existing general benchmarks provide limited evidence of whether a model can apply regulations correctly, perform verifiable engineering calculations, or interpret traffic scenes reliably, while the small number of public transportation benchmarks remain narrow in scope and rarely support fine-grained diagnosis across text, images, and point-cloud data. To address this gap, we present TRIP-Evaluate, an open multimodal benchmark for large models in transportation. The benchmark organizes 837 items using a role-task-knowledge taxonomy that covers vehicle, traffic-management, traveler, and planning-and-design functions. Each item is annotated with capability, modality, and difficulty labels, enabling diagnosis from overall accuracy down to specific failure modes. The current release includes 596 text items, 198 image items, and 43 point-cloud items. TRIP-Evaluate also standardizes item construction, quality control, prompting, decoding, and scoring to improve cross-model comparability. Results on a diverse panel of models show that text-based performance is improving, but substantial weaknesses remain in multi-step engineering calculation, rule-constrained reasoning, multimodal scene understanding, and point-cloud understanding. Overall, TRIP-Evaluate provides a reproducible, diagnosable, and engineering-aligned evaluation baseline for model selection, regression testing, and safer deployment in transportation applications.

AI.tldr: TRIP-Evaluate是一个面向交通领域大模型的开源多模态基准，包含837个按角色-任务-知识分类标注的文本、图像和点云测试项，揭示了当前模型在多步工程计算、规则约束推理和多模态场景理解方面的显著不足。
AI.motivation: 现有通用基准无法评估大模型在交通领域是否具备正确应用法规、执行可验证工程计算或可靠解读交通场景的能力，而少量公开的交通基准范围狭窄、缺乏对文本、图像和点云数据的细粒度诊断能力，难以支持安全关键型交通应用中的模型选择和回归测试。
AI.method: TRIP-Evaluate采用角色-任务-知识分类体系组织837个测试项，覆盖车辆、交通管理、出行者及规划与设计四大功能域，每个项标注能力、模态和难度标签，支持从整体准确率到具体失败模式的诊断；同时标准化了项构建、质量控制、提示、解码和评分流程以提升跨模型可比性，当前版本包含596个文本项、198个图像项和43个点云项。
AI.result: 对多个模型的评测结果显示，文本任务性能正在提升，但在多步工程计算、规则约束推理、多模态场景理解和点云理解方面仍存在显著不足。
AI.conclusion: TRIP-Evaluate为交通领域大模型的模型选择、回归测试和安全部署提供了可复现、可诊断且贴近工程实际的评估基线，揭示了当前模型在安全关键型推理和工程计算上的关键弱点。

title: DiagramNet: An End-to-End Recognition Framework and Dataset for Non-Standard System-Level Diagrams
summary: System-level diagrams encode the architectural blueprint of chip design, specifying module functions, dataflows, and interface protocols. However, non-standardized symbols and the scarcity of structured training data hinder existing multimodal large language models (MLLMs) from recognizing these diagrams. To address this gap, we introduce DiagramNet, the first multimodal dataset for system-level diagrams, comprising 10,977 connection annotations and 15,515 chain-of-thought QA pairs across four tasks: Listing, Localization, Connection, and Circuit QA. Building on this dataset, we propose a progressive training pipeline together with a decoupled multi-agent workflow that decomposes complex visual reasoning into Perception, Reasoning, and Knowledge stages. On the DiagramNet benchmark, integrating our 3B-parameter model with the proposed workflow surpasses the 2025 EDA Elite Challenge winner and outperforms GPT-5, Claude-Sonnet-4, and Gemini-2.5-Pro by over 2x in end-to-end evaluation. Notably, the workflow generalizes beyond our model, boosting Task 1 performance by 128.7x for Gemini-2.5-Pro and 12.4x for GPT-5. Furthermore, with only 60 images for detector adaptation, the method transfers effectively to AMSBench, achieving zero-shot connectivity reasoning on par with GPT-5 and Claude-Sonnet-4 while surpassing the AMS state-of-the-art method Netlistify.

AI.tldr: 本文提出DiagramNet，首个面向系统级电路图的多模态数据集及渐进式训练流程，并结合解耦多智能体工作流（感知-推理-知识三阶段），使3B参数模型在端到端评测中超越GPT-5、Claude-Sonnet-4等大模型，并泛化至AMSBench实现零样本连接推理。
AI.motivation: 系统级电路图使用非标准化符号，且缺乏结构化训练数据，导致现有多模态大语言模型（MLLM）无法有效识别和理解这些图表，亟需一个专门的数据集和推理框架来填补这一空白。
AI.method: 提出DiagramNet数据集，包含10,977条连接标注和15,515条思维链问答对，覆盖Listing、Localization、Connection和Circuit QA四项任务；构建渐进式训练流程，并设计解耦多智能体工作流，将复杂视觉推理拆解为感知（Perception）、推理（Reasoning）和知识（Knowledge）三个阶段。
AI.result: 集成3B参数模型与所提工作流后，在DiagramNet基准上超越2025年EDA精英挑战赛冠军，并在端到端评测中比GPT-5、Claude-Sonnet-4和Gemini-2.5-Pro高出2倍以上；该工作流可泛化至其他模型，使Gemini-2.5-Pro任务1性能提升128.7倍、GPT-5提升12.4倍；仅用60张图像进行检测器适配即可迁移至AMSBench，零样本连接推理性能与GPT-5和Claude-Sonnet-4相当，并超越AMS领域SOTA方法Netlistify。
AI.conclusion: 所提出的数据-训练-推理三阶段框架有效解决了系统级电路图理解中的数据稀缺和符号非标准化问题，且工作流具有强泛化性和数据高效迁移能力，为EDA领域多模态推理提供了可扩展的解决方案。

title: EmoMM: Benchmarking and Steering MLLM for Multimodal Emotion Recognition under Conflict and Missingness
summary: Multimodal Emotion Recognition (MER) is critical for interpreting real-world interactions. While Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLM) have shown promise in MER, their internal decision-making mechanisms under modality conflict and missingness remain largely underexplored. In this paper, to systematically investigate these behaviors, we introduce EmoMM, a comprehensive benchmark featuring modality-aligned, conflict, and missing subsets. Through extensive evaluation, we uncover a Video Contribution Collapse (VCC) phenomenon, where MLLM marginalize video evidence due to high token redundancy and modality preferences. To address this, we propose Conflict-aware Head-level Attention Steering (CHASE), a lightweight mechanism that detects modality conflicts and performs inference-time attention steering, effectively mitigating decision bias without retraining the backbone. Experimental results demonstrate that CHASE consistently improves performance across various settings, significantly enhancing the reliability of MLLM in complex affective scenarios.

AI.tldr: 本文提出 EmoMM 基准和 CHASE 方法，揭示了多模态大语言模型在情感识别中的视频贡献崩溃现象，并通过冲突感知注意力引导机制有效缓解决策偏差。
AI.motivation: 多模态大语言模型在情感识别中虽有潜力，但其在模态冲突和模态缺失情况下的内部决策机制尚未得到充分探索，现有研究缺乏系统性的行为分析方法。
AI.method: 论文构建了 EmoMM 基准，包含模态对齐、模态冲突和模态缺失三个子集；并提出 CHASE（Conflict-aware Head-level Attention Steering）机制，该轻量级方法可在推理时检测模态冲突并引导注意力分布，无需重训骨干网络即可缓解决策偏差。
AI.result: 实验发现 MLLM 存在视频贡献崩溃现象，即因视频令牌冗余和模态偏好导致视频证据被边缘化；CHASE 方法在多种设置下均能一致提升模型性能，显著增强模型在复杂情感场景下的可靠性。
AI.conclusion: 研究表明多模态大语言模型在情感识别中存在显著的模态偏差问题，CHASE 作为一种轻量级推理时干预手段，可有效提升模型在冲突和缺失模态下的鲁棒性，为构建更可靠的多模态情感识别系统提供了新思路。

title: Beyond Perceptual Shortcuts: Causal-Inspired Debiasing Optimization for Generalizable Video Reasoning in Lightweight MLLMs
summary: Although reinforcement learning (RL) has significantly advanced reasoning capabilities in large multimodal language models (MLLMs), its efficacy remains limited for lightweight models essential for edge deployments.To address this issue, we leverage causal analysis and experiment to reveal the underlying phenomenon of perceptual bias, demonstrating that RL-based fine-tuning compels lightweight models to preferentially adopt perceptual shortcuts induced by data biases, rather than developing genuine reasoning abilities.Motivated by this insight, we propose VideoThinker, a causal-inspired framework that cultivates robust reasoning in lightweight models through a two-stage debiasing process. First, the Bias Aware Training stage forges a dedicated "bias model" to embody these shortcut behaviors. Then, the Causal Debiasing Policy Optimization (CDPO) algorithm fine-tunes the primary model, employing an innovative repulsive objective to actively push it away from the bias model's flawed logic while simultaneously pulling it toward correct, generalizable solutions.Our model, VideoThinker-R1, establishes a new state-of-the-art in video reasoning efficiency. For same-scale comparison, requiring no Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and using only 1 of the training data for RL, it surpasses VideoRFT-3B with a 3.2% average gain on widely-used benchmarks and a 7% lead on VideoMME. For cross-scale comparison, it outperforms the larger Video-UTR-7B model on multiple benchmarks, including a 2.1% gain on MVBench and a 3.8% gain on TempCompass. Code is available at https://github.com/falonss703/VideoThinker.

AI.tldr: VideoThinker提出了一种因果启发的去偏框架，通过两阶段训练（Bias Aware Training + Causal Debiasing Policy Optimization）解决轻量级多模态大模型在强化学习微调中因数据偏置而产生感知捷径、无法真正推理的问题，在视频推理任务上以极少数据和无需SFT的方式超越多个同量级和跨量级模型。
AI.motivation: 强化学习虽能提升多模态大模型的推理能力，但对需要部署在边缘设备上的轻量级模型效果有限。现有方法未能解释这一局限性背后的根本原因，导致轻量模型在RL微调后仍难以发展出真实的推理能力。
AI.method: 论文首先通过因果分析和实验揭示轻量模型存在"感知偏置"现象，即RL微调使其倾向于利用数据中的感知捷径而非真正推理。在此基础上提出VideoThinker框架，包含两阶段去偏过程：第一阶段Bias Aware Training构造一个专门的"偏置模型"来显式编码这些捷径行为；第二阶段Causal Debiasing Policy Optimization (CDPO)算法通过新颖的排斥目标函数，在微调主模型时主动使其远离偏置模型的错误逻辑，同时拉向正确的可泛化解。
AI.result: VideoThinker-R1在视频推理任务上达到新的最先进水平。同量级对比中，无需监督微调且仅用1%的RL训练数据，即在多个广泛使用的基准上平均超越VideoRFT-3B达3.2%，在VideoMME上领先7%。跨量级对比中，在多个基准上超越更大的Video-UTR-7B模型，包括在MVBench上提升2.1%，在TempCompass上提升3.8%。
AI.conclusion: 研究揭示了轻量级MLLM在RL微调中因数据偏置产生感知捷径的本质问题，并证明通过因果去偏方法可以有效缓解该问题。VideoThinker框架在极低数据量和无SFT条件下实现了显著的性能提升，为边缘设备上部署高效推理模型提供了可行方案。

title: VoxAfford: Multi-Scale Voxel-Token Fusion for Open-Vocabulary 3D Affordance Detection
summary: Open-vocabulary 3D affordance detection requires localizing interaction regions on point clouds given novel affordance descriptions. Recent methods extend multimodal large language models (MLLMs) with special output tokens that are decoded into segmentation masks. However, these tokens are produced through autoregressive generation, which models sequential dependencies rather than spatial neighborhood relations, leaving them semantically rich but spatially impoverished for 3D localization. We propose Voxel-enhanced Affordance detection (VoxAfford), which bypasses this bottleneck by injecting multi-scale geometric features from a frozen pre-trained 3D VQVAE encoder into the output tokens after generation. Each output token uses its affordance semantics as a query to retrieve relevant geometric patterns from its paired voxel scale via cross-attention, with a learned compatibility gate controlling the injection strength. The enhanced tokens are then aggregated into a spatially-aware affordance prompt through semantic-conditioned attention and propagated alongside per-point features to generate the final mask. Experiments on open-vocabulary affordance detection tasks show that VoxAfford achieves state-of-the-art performance with approximately an 8% improvement in mIoU, and real robot experiments confirm zero-shot transfer to novel objects.

AI.tldr: VoxAfford通过冻结的3D VQVAE编码器注入多尺度几何特征到输出令牌中，利用交叉注意力机制实现语义与几何信息的融合，在开放词汇3D可抓取性检测任务上以约8%的mIoU提升达到最先进性能，并验证了零样本迁移到真实物体的能力。
AI.motivation: 现有基于多模态大语言模型的开放词汇3D可抓取性检测方法通过自回归生成生成特殊输出令牌并解码为分割掩码，但自回归建模仅捕捉序列依赖关系而忽略了空间邻域关系，导致输出令牌虽语义丰富但空间信息贫乏，难以精确地进行3D定位。
AI.method: VoxAfford引入一个冻结的预训练3D VQVAE编码器提取多尺度几何特征，在输出令牌生成后通过交叉注意力机制使每个输出令牌以其可抓取性语义作为查询，从对应体素尺度的几何特征中检索相关空间模式，并利用可学习的兼容性门控机制控制注入强度；增强后的令牌通过语义条件注意力聚合成空间感知的可抓取性提示，再与逐点特征共同传播以生成最终分割掩码。
AI.result: 在开放词汇3D可抓取性检测任务上，VoxAfford取得了最先进性能，平均交并比提升约8%；真实机器人实验验证了该方法对未见物体的零样本迁移能力。
AI.conclusion: VoxAfford通过将预训练3D VQVAE的多尺度几何特征注入MLLM输出令牌，有效解决了自回归生成带来的空间信息不足问题，表明在语义丰富的输出令牌后补充几何先验是一种提升3D定位精度的有效范式，且具有零样本泛化到真实场景的潜力。

title: Video Active Perception: Effective Inference-Time Long-Form Video Understanding with Vision-Language Models
summary: Large vision-language models (VLMs) have advanced multimodal tasks such as video question answering (QA). However, VLMs face the challenge of selecting frames effectively and efficiently, as standard uniform sampling is expensive and performance may plateau. Inspired by active perception theory, which posits that models gain information by acquiring data that differs from their expectations, we introduce Video Active Perception (VAP), a training-free method to enhance long-form video QA using VLMs. Our approach treats keyframe selection as data acquisition in active perception and leverages a lightweight text-conditioned video generation model to represent prior world knowledge. Empirically, VAP achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot results on long-form or reasoning video QA datasets such as EgoSchema, NExT-QA, ActivityNet-QA, IntentQA, and CLEVRER, achieving an increase of up to 5.6 x frame efficiency by frames per question over standard GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5 Pro, and LLaVA-OV. Moreover, VAP shows stronger reasoning abilities than previous methods and effectively selects keyframes relevant to questions. These findings highlight the potential of leveraging active perception to improve the frame effectiveness and efficiency of long-form video QA.

AI.tldr: 提出Video Active Perception (VAP)，一种无需训练的方法，通过将关键帧选择视为主动感知中的数据采集，并利用轻量级文本条件视频生成模型表示先验世界知识，显著提升长视频问答的帧选择效率和推理性能，在多个基准上实现了最高5.6倍的帧效率提升和最先进的零样本结果。
AI.motivation: 现有大视觉语言模型在处理长视频问答时，标准均匀采样策略计算成本高且性能易达到瓶颈，如何有效且高效地选择关键帧是亟待解决的问题。
AI.method: 受主动感知理论启发，VAP将关键帧选择建模为数据采集过程，利用轻量级文本条件视频生成模型作为先验世界知识，无需额外训练即可筛选出与问题最相关的帧。
AI.result: 在EgoSchema、NExT-QA、ActivityNet-QA、IntentQA和CLEVRER等多个长视频或推理型视频QA数据集上，VAP取得了最先进的零样本结果，相较标准GPT-4o、Gemini 1.5 Pro和LLaVA-OV，每问题帧效率提升高达5.6倍。
AI.conclusion: VAP证明了利用主动感知原理可以显著提升长视频QA中帧选择的有效性和效率，展现出比以往方法更强的推理能力和关键帧选取能力，为高效视频理解提供了新思路。

title: Chart-FR1: Visual Focus-Driven Fine-Grained Reasoning on Dense Charts
summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have shown considerable potential in chart understanding and reasoning tasks. However, they still struggle with high information density (HID) charts characterized by multiple subplots, legends, and dense annotations due to three major challenges: (1) limited fine-grained perception results in the omission of critical visual cues; (2) redundant or noisy visual information undermines the performance of multimodal reasoning; (3) lack of adaptive deep reasoning relative to the amount of visual information. To tackle these challenges, we present a novel focus-driven fine-grained chart reasoning model, Chart-FR1, to improve perception, focusing efficiency, and adaptive deep reasoning on HID charts. Specifically, we propose Focus-CoT, a visual focusing chain-of-thought that enhances fine-grained perception by explicitly linking reasoning steps to key visual cues, such as local image regions and OCR signals. Building on this, we introduce Focus-GRPO, a focus-driven reinforcement learning algorithm with an information-efficiency reward that compresses redundant visual information for efficient focusing, and an adaptive KL penalty mechanism that enables flexible control over reasoning depth as more visual cues are discovered. Furthermore, to fill the gap in benchmarks for HID charts, we build HID-Chart, a challenging benchmark with an information-density metric designed to evaluate fine-grained chart reasoning capabilities. Extensive experiments on multiple chart benchmarks demonstrate that Chart-FR1 outperforms state-of-the-art MLLMs in chart understanding and reasoning. Code is available at https://github.com/phkhub/Chart-FR1.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Chart-FR1，一种基于焦点驱动与细粒度推理的图表理解模型，通过Focus-CoT、Focus-GRPO和HID-Chart基准分别解决高信息密度图表中的细粒度感知、冗余信息压缩和自适应深度推理问题，在多个图表基准上超越现有多模态大模型。
AI.motivation: 现有MLLM在高信息密度（HID）图表上存在三大瓶颈：细粒度感知能力不足导致遗漏关键视觉线索；冗余或噪声视觉信息削弱多模态推理性能；缺乏能根据视觉信息量自适应调整的深度推理机制。此外，针对HID图表的评测基准也存在空白。
AI.method: 提出Chart-FR1模型，包含三个核心组件：Focus-CoT（视觉聚焦链式推理），通过将推理步骤显式链接到局部图像区域和OCR信号等关键视觉线索来增强细粒度感知；Focus-GRPO，一种焦点驱动的强化学习算法，引入信息效率奖励以压缩冗余视觉信息，并设计自适应KL惩罚机制以根据发现的视觉线索灵活控制推理深度；以及HID-Chart基准，配备信息密度度量指标用于评估细粒度图表推理能力。
AI.result: 在多个图表推理基准上的大量实验表明，Chart-FR1在图表理解和推理任务上全面超越现有最先进的多模态大模型。
AI.conclusion: 本文验证了聚焦式视觉推理与自适应深度推理机制在高信息密度图表理解中的有效性，为MLLM在处理复杂结构化视觉信息时提供了一种兼顾感知精细度与推理效率的新范式，并为该领域提供了专门的评测基准。

title: Perceptual Flow Network for Visually Grounded Reasoning
comment: 36 pages with 17 figures, Accepted at ICML 2026
summary: Despite the success of Large-Vision Language Models (LVLMs), general optimization objectives (e.g., standard MLE) fail to constrain visual trajectories, leading to language bias and hallucination. To mitigate this, current methods introduce geometric priors from visual experts as additional supervision. However, we observe that such supervision is typically suboptimal: it is biased toward geometric precision and offers limited reasoning utility. To bridge this gap, we propose Perceptual Flow Network (PFlowNet), which eschews rigid alignment with the expert priors and achieves interpretable yet more effective visual reasoning. Specifically, PFlowNet decouples perception from reasoning to establish a self-conditioned generation process. Based on this, it integrates multi-dimensional rewards with vicinal geometric shaping via variational reinforcement learning, thereby facilitating reasoning-oriented perceptual behaviors while preserving visual reliability. PFlowNet delivers a provable performance guarantee and competitive empirical results, particularly setting new SOTA records on V* Bench (90.6%) and MME-RealWorld-lite (67.0%).

AI.tldr: PFlowNet通过解耦感知与推理并引入变分强化学习，在无需严格对齐视觉专家先验的情况下实现了可解释且更有效的视觉推理，在V* Bench和MME-RealWorld-lite上取得了新的最优结果。
AI.motivation: 现有大视觉语言模型（LVLMs）在标准MLE优化下存在视觉轨迹受限、语言偏置和幻觉问题，而当前引入视觉专家几何先验作为额外监督的方法偏向几何精度且推理效用有限，亟需一种不依赖刚性对齐且更具推理能力的视觉优化范式。
AI.method: PFlowNet提出感知与推理解耦的自条件生成过程，并在此基础上通过变分强化学习整合多维奖励与邻域几何塑造，从而在保持视觉可靠性的同时促进面向推理的感知行为。
AI.result: PFlowNet在V* Bench上达到90.6%，在MME-RealWorld-lite上达到67.0%，均刷新了当时的最高记录，同时提供了可证明的性能保障。
AI.conclusion: 该研究表明，放弃与视觉专家先验的刚性对齐、转而通过解耦架构与变分强化学习引导感知行为，能够同时提升视觉推理的可解释性和有效性，为后续LVLMs的视觉优化提供了新方向。

title: From Where Things Are to What They Are For: Benchmarking Spatial-Functional Intelligence in Multimodal LLMs
summary: Human-level agentic intelligence extends beyond low-level geometric perception, evolving from recognizing where things are to understanding what they are for. While existing benchmarks effectively evaluate the geometric perception capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs), they fall short of probing the higher-order cognitive abilities required for grounded intelligence. To address this gap, we introduce the Spatial-Functional Intelligence Benchmark (SFI-Bench), a video-based benchmark with over 1,500 expert-annotated questions derived from diverse egocentric indoor video scans. SFI-Bench systematically evaluates two complementary dimensions of advanced reasoning: (1) Structured Spatial Reasoning, which requires understanding complex layouts and forming coherent spatial representations, and (2) Functional Reasoning, which involves inferring object affordances and their context-dependent utility. The benchmark includes tasks such as conditional counting, multi-hop relational reasoning, functional pairing, and knowledge-grounded troubleshooting, directly challenging models to integrate perception, memory, and inference. Our experiments reveal that current MLLMs consistently struggle to combine spatial memory with functional reasoning and external knowledge, highlighting a critical bottleneck in achieving grounded intelligence. SFI-Bench therefore provides a diagnostic tool for measuring progress toward more cognitively capable and truly grounded multimodal agents.

AI.tldr: 本文提出SFI-Bench，一个基于视频的基准测试，包含1,500多条专家标注问题，系统评估多模态大模型在结构化空间推理与功能推理两方面的能力。实验表明当前MLLMs在整合空间记忆、功能推理与外部知识方面存在显著瓶颈，揭示了实现具身智能的关键障碍。
AI.motivation: 现有基准测试主要评估多模态大模型的几何感知能力，但缺乏对高阶认知能力（如空间理解与功能推理）的系统性评测，无法有效衡量模型向具身智能发展的进展。
AI.method: SFI-Bench基于第一人称室内视频扫描构建，包含1,500多条专家标注问题，从结构化空间推理（空间布局理解与空间表征构建）和功能推理（物体可供性与上下文效用推断）两个互补维度设计任务，包括条件计数、多跳关系推理、功能配对和知识导向的故障排除等。
AI.result: 实验表明，当前多模态大模型在整合空间记忆、功能推理与外部知识方面持续表现不佳，暴露了实现具身智能的关键瓶颈。
AI.conclusion: SFI-Bench可作为衡量多模态智能体认知能力进展的诊断工具，指出了当前MLLMs在更高层次认知推理上的系统性不足，为迈向真正具身的多模态智能体提供了评估方向。

title: Metric Unreliability in Multimodal Machine Unlearning: A Systematic Analysis and Principled Unified Score
summary: Machine unlearning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) is required for compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), yet current evaluation practices are inconsistent. We present the first systematic study of metric reliability in multimodal unlearning. Five standard metrics, Forget Accuracy (FA), Retain Accuracy (RA), Membership Inference Attack (MIA), Activation Distance (AD), and JS divergence (JS), yield conflicting method rankings across three VQA benchmarks (MLLMU-Bench, UnLOK-VQA, MMUBench). Kendall tau analysis over 36 unlearned LLaVA-1.5-7B models reveals two opposing clusters, {FA, RA, MIA} and {AD, JS}, with tau_FA_AD = -0.26, reproduced on BLIP-2 OPT-2.7B. Agreement is lower in multimodal VQA (average tau = 0.086) than in unimodal classification (average tau = 0.158; difference = 0.072), indicating that dual image-and-text pathways amplify inconsistency. We introduce the Unified Quality Score (UQS), a composite metric with weights derived from each metric's Spearman correlation with the oracle distance d(M_hat, M_star), where M_star is the oracle model retrained only on the retain set. RA shows the strongest reliability (rho = 0.484, p = 0.003), while FA is negatively correlated (rho = -0.418, p = 0.011). UQS yields stable rankings under 100 random weight perturbations (tau = 0.647 +- 0.262). We release the benchmark, 36 checkpoints, and an interactive leaderboard. Code and pre-computed results are available at https://github.com/neurips26/UnifiedUnl.

AI.tldr: 该论文首次系统研究多模态机器遗忘中评估指标的一致性，发现现有指标在视觉-语言模型上存在显著冲突，并提出统一质量评分（UQS）作为稳定可靠的替代方案。
AI.motivation: 视觉-语言模型（VLM）中的机器遗忘对于满足GDPR合规要求至关重要，但当前评估实践缺乏一致性，不同指标在跨模态场景下可能给出相互矛盾的模型排名，这一问题尚未被系统研究。
AI.method: 论文在三个VQA基准（MLLMU-Bench, UnLOK-VQA, MMUBench）上使用五个标准指标（FA、RA、MIA、AD、JS）对36个遗忘后的LLaVA-1.5-7B模型进行评估，通过Kendall tau分析和Spearman相关性分析衡量指标间一致性，并提出UQS（Unified Quality Score）复合指标，其权重基于各指标与Oracle距离的Spearman相关性确定。
AI.result: Kendall tau分析揭示{FA, RA, MIA}和{AD, JS}两个对立的指标聚类（tau_FA_AD = -0.26），且在BLIP-2 OPT-2.7B上复现；多模态VQA场景下的指标平均一致性（tau=0.086）低于单模态分类场景（tau=0.158）；RA的可靠性最高（rho=0.484, p=0.003），FA呈负相关（rho=-0.418, p=0.011）；UQS在100次随机权重扰动下保持稳定排名（tau=0.647±0.262）。
AI.conclusion: 多模态遗忘评估中存在由图像-文本双路径导致的系统性指标不一致问题，RA是最可靠的单一指标，而UQS提供了稳定的综合评估方案；研究同时公开了基准、36个检查点和交互式排行榜以推动标准化评估。

title: Retrieving Any Relevant Moments: Benchmark and Models for Generalized Moment Retrieval
comment: Code and dataset: https://github.com/dymm9977/generalized-moment-retrieval. Keywords: video moment retrieval, temporal grounding, benchmark, multi-modal learning
summary: Video Moment Retrieval (VMR) aims to localize temporal segments in videos that correspond to a natural language query, but typically assumes only a single matching moment for each query. This assumption does not always hold in real-world scenarios, where queries may correspond to multiple or no moments. Thus, we formulate Generalized Moment Retrieval (GMR), a unified setting that requires retrieving the complete set of relevant moments or predicting an empty set. To enable systematic study of GMR, we introduce Soccer-GMR, a large-scale benchmark built on challenging soccer videos that reflect general GMR scenarios, with realistic negative and positive queries. The benchmark is constructed via a duration-flexible semi-automated pipeline with human verification, enabling scalable data generation while maintaining high annotation quality. We further design a unified evaluation protocol with complementary metrics tailored for null-set rejection, positive-query localization, and end-to-end GMR performance. Finally, we establish strong baselines across two modeling paradigms: a lightweight plug-and-play GMR adapter for discriminative VMR models, and a GMR-tailored GRPO reward for fine-tuning multimodal large language models (MLLMs). Extensive experiments show consistent gains across all metrics and expose key limitations of current methods, positioning GMR as a more realistic and challenging benchmark for video-language understanding.

AI.tldr: 本文提出广义视频时刻检索（GMR）任务，统一处理查询对应多个相关时刻或空集的情况，并构建了大规模足球视频基准Soccer-GMR，设计统一评估协议及两种建模范式基线，实验表明该方法有效且揭示了现有方法的局限性。
AI.motivation: 现有视频时刻检索（VMR）方法假设每个查询仅对应一个匹配时刻，但在真实场景中查询可能对应多个相关时刻或没有任何匹配时刻，这一假设限制了方法的实用性。本文旨在解决这一研究空白，将VMR推广为广义时刻检索（GMR）任务，要求模型能够检索完整的相关时刻集合或预测空集。
AI.method: 本文构建了Soccer-GMR基准数据集，采用时长灵活的半自动标注流程，结合人工验证实现可扩展的高质量数据生成。针对GMR任务设计了统一的评估协议，包含拒绝空集、正查询定位和端到端GMR三种互补指标。提出了两种建模范式基线：一种轻量级即插即用GMR适配器用于判别式VMR模型，以及一种专为GMR设计的GRPO奖励函数用于微调多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）。
AI.result: 大量实验表明，所提出的方法在所有评估指标上均取得一致性提升，同时揭示了现有方法在GMR设定下的关键局限性，证明GMR是一个更现实且更具挑战性的视频-语言理解基准。
AI.conclusion: 本文通过定义GMR任务、构建Soccer-GMR基准并建立基线方法，推动了视频时刻检索向更贴近实际应用场景的方向发展，为视频-语言理解研究提供了更严格的评估框架和新的研究方向。
